options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: packname
options.description:
The modpack to be used in game 
Installed packs: 
bob's: InternalPack
vanilla: InternalPack

options.name: number_of_science_packs
options.description:
Number of science packs required for research and to complete the game.
Gets caped by the maximum number of science packs in the game.

options.name: additional_rocket_pool
options.description:
Adds a pool to draw items from specifically for the rocket, silo and satellite randomized recipes.
This is to emulate the Vanilla AP factorio space_science_pack setting but for all Maximum Science Packs.
It Doesn't affect the difficulty of the rocket but lowers the difficulty of science packs slightly.

options.name: percent_items_in_game
options.description:
The percentage of items in the game considered for randomized recipes.
Lowering will cause all ingame recipes to become easier.
If generation fails, try upping this.

options.name: no_earlier_pools
options.description:
When enabled when randomizing recipes, no earlier item pools will be considered for ingredients.
This will drastically increase the difficulty you've been warned.

options.name: goal
options.description: Goal required to complete the game.

options.name: silo
options.description: Ingredients to craft rocket silo or auto-place if set to spawn.

options.name: satellite
options.description: Ingredients to craft satellite.

options.name: free_samples
options.description: Get free items with your technologies.

options.name: free_samples_quality
options.description:
If free samples are on, determine the quality of the granted items.
Requires the quality mod, which is part of the Space Age DLC. Without it, normal quality is given.

options.name: starting_items
options.description: Mapping of Factorio internal item-name to amount granted on start.

options.name: free_sample_blacklist
options.description: Set of items that should never be granted from Free Samples

options.name: free_sample_whitelist
options.description: Overrides any free sample blacklist present. This may ruin the balance of the mod, be warned.

options.name: recipe_time
options.description:
Randomize the time it takes for any recipe to craft, this includes smelting, chemical lab, hand crafting etc.
Fast: 0.25X - 1X
Normal: 0.5X - 2X
Slow: 1X - 4X
Chaos: 0.25X - 4X
New category: ignores vanilla recipe time and rolls new one
New Fast: 0.25 - 2 seconds
New Normal: 0.25 - 10 seconds
New Slow:  5 - 10 seconds

options.name: recipe_ingredients
options.description: Select if rocket, or rocket + science pack ingredients should be random.

options.name: recipe_ingredients_offset
options.description:
When randomizing ingredients, remove or add this many "slots" of items.
For example, at -1 a randomized Automation Science Pack will only require 1 ingredient, instead of 2.

options.name: imported_blueprints
options.description: Allow or Disallow Blueprints from outside the current savegame.

options.name: world_gen
options.description:
World gen settings for the factorio world
If you use custom, you must define the logic in custom_world_generation
World Generation settings. Overview of options at https://wiki.factorio.com/Map_generator,
with in-depth documentation at https://lua-api.factorio.com/latest/concepts/MapGenSettings.html
An example for this can be found: https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1426234278462750860/1459637472496586905

options.name: custom_world_gen
options.description:
World Generation settings. Overview of options at https://wiki.factorio.com/Map_generator,
with in-depth documentation at https://lua-api.factorio.com/latest/concepts/MapGenSettings.html

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: energy_link
options.description: Allow sending energy to other worlds. 25% of the energy is lost in the transfer.

options.name: additional_logic
options.description:
Add additional logic that can make progression nicer.
If you use custom, you must define the logic in custom_additional_logic
An example for this can be found: https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1426234278462750860/1437568465987829975

options.name: custom_additional_logic
options.description:
Custom logic: hidden
example found here: https://discord.com/channels/731205301247803413/1426234278462750860/1437568465987829975

options.name: tech_tree_layout
options.description:
Selects how the tech tree nodes are interwoven.
Single: No dependencies
Diamonds: Several grid graphs (4/9/16 nodes each)
Pyramids: Several top halves of diamonds (6/10/15 nodes each)
Funnels: Several bottom halves of diamonds (6/10/15 nodes each)
Trees: Several trees
Choices: A single balanced binary tree
Irregular: A single randomly generated technology tree.

options.name: min_tech_cost
options.description: The cheapest a Technology can be in Science Packs.

options.name: max_tech_cost
options.description: The most expensive a Technology can be in Science Packs.

options.name: tech_cost_distribution
options.description:
Random distribution of costs of the Science Packs.
Even: any number between min and max is equally likely.
Low: low costs, near the minimum, are more likely.
Middle: medium costs, near the average, are more likely.
High: high costs, near the maximum, are more likely.

options.name: tech_cost_mix
options.description:
Percent chance that a preceding Science Pack is also required.
Chance is rolled per preceding pack.

options.name: ramping_tech_costs
options.description:
Forces the amount of Science Packs required to ramp up with the highest involved Pack. Average is preserved.
For example:
off: Automation (red)/Logistics (green) sciences can range from 1 to 1000 Science Packs,
on: Automation (red) ranges to ~500 packs and Logistics (green) from ~500 to 1000 Science Packs

options.name: tech_layer_obscurity
options.description:
Hides technologies based on what science packs you have crafted.
on: If you have crafted Automation (red) science. You can only view technologies that require Automation (red) science.
off: Even if you have crafted only Automation (red) science. You can still see technologies that require Utility (yellow) science.
WARNING: no technology will be visible at the start of the game. You need to craft a red science pack to view any technologies.
If information is Full, then this will also hide the hints.
Works alone or in combination with Depth Obscurity.

options.name: tech_depth_obscurity
options.description:
Hides technologies based on what you have researched.
The value determines how far you can see.
Tech tree example:
   B   |
   |   |
A  C   |
|\ |  /
D  E
|
F
Only A is researched.
0: disabled. This feature shuts of at 0.
1: You only see sciences that are one step away from what you have researched so far. In the example above. This will be B and D. (Note, E needs C to be researched.)
2: You will see techs that are two steps away from all the tech you have researched. In the example above this is A, B, C, D and F. (E is still not visible.)
If information is Full, then this will also hide the hints.
Works alone or in combination with Layer Obscurity.

options.name: tech_tree_information
options.description:
How much information should be displayed in the tech tree.
None: No indication of what a research unlocks.
Advancement: Indicates if a research unlocks an item that is considered logical advancement, but not who it is for.
Full only hint advancement: Labels with exact names and recipients of unlocked items; only the advancement unlocks are prefilled into the !hint command, or get hinted out by the tech obscurity.
Full: Labels with exact names and recipients of unlocked items; all researches are prefilled into the !hint command, or get hinted out by the tech obscurity.

options.name: progressive
options.description:
Merges together Technologies like "automation-1" to "automation-3" into 3 copies of "Progressive Automation",
which awards them in order.

options.name: teleport_traps
options.description:
Trap items that when received trigger a random teleport.
It is ensured the player can walk back to where they got teleported from.

options.name: grenade_traps
options.description: Trap items that when received trigger a grenade explosion on each player.

options.name: cluster_grenade_traps
options.description: Trap items that when received trigger a cluster grenade explosion on each player.

options.name: artillery_traps
options.description: Trap items that when received trigger an artillery shell on each player.

options.name: atomic_rocket_traps
options.description:
Trap items that when received trigger an atomic rocket explosion on each player.
Warning: there is no warning. The launch is instantaneous.

options.name: atomic_cliff_remover_traps
options.description:
Trap items that when received trigger an atomic rocket explosion on a random cliff.
Warning: there is no warning. The launch is instantaneous.

options.name: inventory_spill_traps
options.description: Trap items that when received trigger dropping your main inventory and trash inventory onto the ground.

options.name: attack_traps
options.description: Trap items that when received trigger an attack on your base.

options.name: evolution_traps
options.description: Trap items that when received increase the enemy evolution.

options.name: evolution_trap_increase
options.description:
How much an Evolution Trap increases the enemy evolution.
Increases scale down proportionally to the session's current evolution factor
(40 increase at 0.50 will add 0.20... 40 increase at 0.75 will add 0.10...)

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
